blueprint_refs: execution: Two supported generation modes (A) Calibrated generative sequential model ... (B) Optional LLM pipeline (Pattern/Persona/Motivation)

evidence.metrics: Execution succeeded; this is an architecture/scope mismatch rather than a metric failure.

llm-review-pipeline-not-in-blueprint.error_identification: Code contains an unrelated product-review LLM pipeline and OpenAI client scaffolding that is never used by the mobility simulator.
llm-review-pipeline-not-in-blueprint.root_cause_analysis: These classes/functions are not part of either blueprint Mode A (calibrated sequential model) or Mode B (Pattern/Persona/Motivation for mobility). Keeping unrelated LLM code increases maintenance risk and can confuse future iterations.
llm-review-pipeline-not-in-blueprint.correct_approach: Remove the review-focused LLM pipeline code paths entirely, or if an LLM mode is desired, replace with a mobility-specific Pattern/Persona/Motivation implementation that consumes TRAIN-only summaries and emits strict trajectory strings through TrajectoryStringFormatter.
llm-review-pipeline-not-in-blueprint.key_insight: Keep generated code tightly aligned to the simulation blueprint; delete unrelated agent pipelines to avoid scope drift and accidental dependency/runtime issues.

evidence.metrics: Reported metrics are: category_share_mae, stop_count_abs_mean_error, stop_count_kl, tod_jsd_avg, topk_poi_recall, transition_divergence, trip_distance_wasserstein (do not match blueprint metric names).

metrics-mismatch-vs-blueprint-required-k.error_identification: Evaluation is not implementing the blueprint-specified metric set/names; instead it uses a different set (category_share_mae/topk_poi_recall/etc.). This makes calibration optimize a different objective than required by spec.
metrics-mismatch-vs-blueprint-required-k.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator.compute_metrics computes alternative metrics and the calibration controller optimizes those. Blueprint requires (at minimum) events_per_day_distribution_distance, step_distance_km_distribution_distance, and time_of_day_histogram_jsd_by_category.
metrics-mismatch-vs-blueprint-required-k.correct_approach: Refactor Evaluator to compute and output the blueprint metrics (including correct naming). Map existing internals: stop-count distribution distance (use Wasserstein/KL as allowed) -> events_per_day_distribution_distance; trip distances -> step_distance_km_distribution_distance; time-of-day JSD should be by category (return per-category and average) -> time_of_day_histogram_jsd_by_category. Ensure calibration objective uses ONLY these blueprint metrics on validation.
metrics-mismatch-vs-blueprint-required-k.key_insight: Calibration is only as good as the metric contract: match blueprint metric names/definitions exactly or you will optimize the wrong thing.

blueprint_refs: policy_form: (1) Determine planned number of events for the day (either directly modeled or via calibrated distribution).
stop-count-model-causes-large-stop-count.error_identification: Stop counts are significantly miscalibrated: average stop count differs by ~2.88 and the distribution KL is high (1.45).
stop-count-model-causes-large-stop-count.root_cause_analysis: Stop counts are generated by multiplying a sampled historical count by a global scale (stop_count_scale) and rounding. This single-parameter scaling cannot match both mean and distribution shape across agents/days, and it ignores day-of-week/day-type effects mentioned in the blueprint.
stop-count-model-causes-large-stop-count.correct_approach: Replace scalar scaling with an explicit stop-count distribution model: fit per-agent (or hierarchical) discrete distribution on TRAIN (e.g., empirical PMF with smoothing) and sample directly; optionally condition on day-of-week. Keep stop_count_scale only as a minor global adjustment or remove it. If retaining calibration, calibrate distribution temperature or mixture with global PMF rather than a scale multiplier.
stop-count-model-causes-large-stop-count.key_insight: For discrete count targets, scaling a sampled count is a weak surrogate; model the count distribution directly (and condition on covariates like day-of-week) to reduce both mean error and KL.

blueprint_refs: policy_form: Choose specific POI using utility combining ... distance decay ... agent preferences
poi-choice-uses-truncated-category-candi.error_identification: The simulator almost never visits agents’ true frequent POIs: top-k POI recall is ~0.087.
poi-choice-uses-truncated-category-candi.root_cause_analysis: When a category has many POIs, candidates are truncated to the first 300 IDs in env.category_to_poi_ids without any ranking by agent preference, proximity, or transition likelihood. This can exclude an agent’s actual preferred POIs from consideration, making recall structurally impossible for dense categories.
poi-choice-uses-truncated-category-candi.correct_approach: When subsampling candidates, sample/construct candidates using agent-informed scores: include all agent-seen POIs in that category (from priors.poi_prob keys) plus a proximity-based shortlist (closest N to current location/home) plus a small random exploration set. Only then compute utilities and softmax. Do not slice the first 300 deterministically.
poi-choice-uses-truncated-category-candi.key_insight: Never truncate candidate sets with an arbitrary fixed prefix; candidate generation must preserve high-probability items under the agent’s preference/transition model or you cap achievable recall.

blueprint_refs: transition_model_params: Estimate personal and global transition probabilities ... with smoothing.
poi-transition-model-support-too-narrow.error_identification: Despite low category-level transition divergence, POI-level realism is poor (low POI recall), suggesting POI transition dynamics are not helping choose correct places.
poi-transition-model-support-too-narrow.root_cause_analysis: poi_trans_prob is built only over seen_pois (= POIs observed in TRAIN for that agent). But _choose_poi may consider candidates outside seen_pois (all POIs in a category). For these, the transition term falls back to 1e-12, effectively removing transition structure and pushing the model toward preference-only or distance-only behavior. Combined with candidate truncation, this harms POI fidelity.
poi-transition-model-support-too-narrow.correct_approach: Back off POI transitions with a smoothed mixture: if (prev_poi, poi_id) missing, use (prev_cat, cat) transition or category-conditioned POI preference (priors.poi_prob) rather than a near-zero constant. Alternatively, build category-level transition model only and avoid sparse POI->POI transitions unless you can build a broader support (e.g., top-M POIs per category per agent).
poi-transition-model-support-too-narrow.key_insight: Sparse POI-to-POI transitions need a principled backoff (to category transitions or preferences); using a near-zero fallback silently disables the feature and degrades downstream choice quality.

blueprint_refs: time_of_day_priors_by_category: build histogram ... used for time-conditioned scheduling/choice
time-of-day-alignment-mechanism-too-weak.error_identification: Time-of-day match is only moderate (tod_jsd_avg ~0.079), and evaluation does not provide per-category JSD required by blueprint.
time-of-day-alignment-mechanism-too-weak.root_cause_analysis: Category choice uses tod_prob at current time bin and a sampled target minute, but only lightly penalizes misalignment (constant -2.0 * normalized abs diff). Arrival time is then forced by max(current+travel, target_min), which can jump arbitrarily and distort timing patterns. Also, evaluator reports only an average JSD without per-category breakdown demanded by the blueprint.
time-of-day-alignment-mechanism-too-weak.correct_approach: Separate (a) selecting category given current time (use tod_prob only), and (b) scheduling the next event time using a learned inter-event gap model or category-specific start-time sampling anchored by current_time (e.g., sample a future minute from a conditional distribution over (minute-current_time) rather than absolute minute). In evaluation, output per-category JSD map plus the average, matching blueprint metric name/structure.
time-of-day-alignment-mechanism-too-weak.key_insight: Time realism improves when you model forward-looking time increments (gaps) rather than sampling absolute target times and snapping; always expose per-segment diagnostics (per-category JSD) to localize timing failures.

blueprint_refs: exogenous_signals: day_of_week modulates category/time-of-day priors and transitions (weekday vs weekend).
day-of-week-signal-not-used.error_identification: Model does not condition behavior on day type, likely contributing to stop-count and time-of-day mismatches.
day-of-week-signal-not-used.root_cause_analysis: Although the blueprint requires day-of-week modulation, the parsed DayLog does not store day-of-week, priors are not learned conditionally, and simulate_one_day ignores target_day’s weekday/weekend.
day-of-week-signal-not-used.correct_approach: Compute day_of_week from date during parsing and learn separate priors/histograms (and optionally stop-count PMFs) for weekday vs weekend (or full 7-day). At simulation time, select the appropriate conditional priors based on target_day.
day-of-week-signal-not-used.key_insight: If the spec includes an exogenous covariate (day-of-week), it must be threaded through parsing → priors → simulation; otherwise you average over regimes and degrade realism.

evidence.metrics: Run succeeded, but this is a robustness/packaging issue.

script-executes-on-import.error_identification: The script calls main() unconditionally at import time, which makes it unsafe to import for testing/reuse.
script-executes-on-import.root_cause_analysis: Missing the standard guard `if __name__ == '__main__': main()`; current code ends with `main()`.
script-executes-on-import.correct_approach: Wrap main invocation in an __name__ guard to prevent accidental execution when imported.
script-executes-on-import.key_insight: Always protect CLI entrypoints with an __name__ guard to enable unit testing and library reuse without side effects.
